randomNumber:
:- module random_number_demo.
:- interface.
:- import_module io.
:- pred main(io::di, io::uo) is det.

:- implementation.
:- import_module random, int, float, list, maybe, string.

main(!IO) :-
    % Initialize the random number generator.
    % This uses an impure C interface internally for seeding by default,
    % or you can manage the random state explicitly.
    random.init(MaybeRandGen, !IO),
    (
        MaybeRandGen = yes(RandGen0),
        io.write_string("\n--- Using Initialized Generator --- \n", !IO),

        % Generate random integer in [Min, Max]
        MinInt = 1, MaxInt = 100,
        random.generate_random_int_between(MinInt, MaxInt, RandomInt, RandGen0, RandGen1),
        io.format("Random Int (%d-%d): %d\n", [i(MinInt), i(MaxInt), i(RandomInt)], !IO),

        % Generate random float in [0.0, 1.0)
        random.generate_random_float(RandomFloat, RandGen1, RandGen2),
        io.format("Random Float [0.0, 1.0): %f\n", [f(RandomFloat)], !IO),

        % Generate random float in [Min, Max) - scale manually
        MinF = 10.0, MaxF = 20.0,
        random.generate_random_float(RF01, RandGen2, RandGen3),
        RandomFloatRange = MinF + (MaxF - MinF) * RF01,
        io.format("Random Float [%f, %f): %f\n", [f(MinF), f(MaxF), f(RandomFloatRange)], !IO),

        % Generate random boolean (using int 0 or 1)
        random.generate_random_int_between(0, 1, RandBoolInt, RandGen3, RandGen4),
        ( if RandBoolInt = 0 then BoolStr = "false" else BoolStr = "true" ),
        io.format("Random Bool: %s\n", [s(BoolStr)], !IO),

        % Select random element from a list
        Fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"],
        ( if list.empty(Fruits) then
            io.write_string("List is empty.\n", !IO),
            RandGen = RandGen4 % Propagate state
        else
            random.generate_random_int_between(0, list.length(Fruits) - 1, Index, RandGen4, RandGen5),
            list.det_index0(Fruits, Index, RandomFruit), % Get element at Index
            io.format("Random Fruit from %s: %s\n", [s(string(Fruits)), s(RandomFruit)], !IO),
            RandGen = RandGen5 % Propagate state
        ),
        _ = RandGen % Consume final state if not used further

    ;
        MaybeRandGen = no,
        io.write_string("Failed to initialize random number generator.\n", !IO)
    ).


    % Using impure predicates for convenience (less common in pure Mercury code)
    % These handle the state implicitly but are marked 'impure'
    % io.write_string("\n--- Using Impure Predicates --- \n", !IO),
    % random.impure_random_int_between(1, 100, ImpureInt, !IO),
    % io.format("Impure Random Int: %d\n", [i(ImpureInt)], !IO),
    % random.impure_random_float(ImpureFloat, !IO),
    % io.format("Impure Random Float: %f\n", [f(ImpureFloat)], !IO).

:- module reverse_string_demo.

fibonacci:
:- module fibonacci_demo.
:- interface.
:- import_module io.
:- pred main(io::di, io::uo) is det.

:- implementation.
:- import_module int, list, string, require, exception.
:- import_moduleரம் % Using bigint from gmp library

% Predicate to calculate the Nth Fibonacci number (0-indexed) iteratively
:- pred fibonacci(int::in, bigint::out) is det.
fibonacci(N, Fib) :-
    require(N >= 0, "fibonacci: input must be non-negative"),
    ( if N = 0 then
        Fib = bigint.zero
    else if N = 1 then
        Fib = bigint.one
    else
        fibonacci_loop(2, N, bigint.zero, bigint.one, Fib)
    ).

% Helper for the loop
:- pred fibonacci_loop(int::in, int::in, bigint::in, bigint::in, bigint::out) is det.
fibonacci_loop(I, N, A, B, Fib) :-
    ( if I > N then
        Fib = B
    else
        Next = A + B,
        fibonacci_loop(I + 1, N, B, Next, Fib)
    ).

% Function to generate the first 'count' Fibonacci numbers
:- func generate_fibonacci_sequence(int) = list(bigint).
generate_fibonacci_sequence(Count) = Sequence :-
    require(Count >= 0, "generate_fibonacci_sequence: count must be non-negative"),
    Indices = list.init(Count, (func(I) = I)), % Create list [0, 1, ..., Count-1]
    Sequence = list.map(fibonacci, Indices). % Apply fibonacci to each index


main(!IO) :-
    N = 10,
    fibonacci(N, FibN),
    io.format("Fibonacci number F(%d): %s\n", [i(N), s(bigint.to_string(FibN))], !IO),

    Count = 15,
    Sequence = generate_fibonacci_sequence(Count),
    SequenceStrings = list.map(bigint.to_string, Sequence), % Convert BigInts to Strings
    SequenceStr = string.join_list(", ", SequenceStrings), % Join strings
    io.format("First %d Fibonacci numbers: [%s]\n", [i(Count), s(SequenceStr)], !IO),

    LargeN = 90,
    fibonacci(LargeN, FibLargeN),
    io.format("Fibonacci number F(%d): %s\n", [i(LargeN), s(bigint.to_string(FibLargeN))], !IO),

    % Example error handling
    ( try [io(!IO)] (
        fibonacci(-1, _)
      )
      catch_any E ->
        io.write_string("Caught expected error: ", !IO),
        io.write(E, !IO),
        io.nl(!IO)
    ).
factorial:
:- module factorial_demo.
:- interface.
:- import_module io.
:- pred main(io::di, io::uo) is det.

:- implementation.
:- import_module int, string, require, exception.
:- import_moduleரம் % Using bigint from gmp library

% Predicate to calculate factorial N! iteratively
:- pred factorial(int::in, bigint::out) is det.
factorial(N, Fact) :-
    require(N >= 0, "factorial: N must be non-negative"),
    factorial_loop(N, bigint.one, Fact). % Start loop with initial result 1

% Tail-recursive helper loop
:- pred factorial_loop(int::in, bigint::in, bigint::out) is det.
factorial_loop(N, Acc, Fact) :-
    ( if N =< 0 then % Base case: 0! or 1! is handled by initial Acc=1
        Fact = Acc
    else
        % Convert N to BigInt for multiplication
        NBig = bigint.from_int(N),
        NewAcc = Acc * NBig,
        factorial_loop(N - 1, NewAcc, Fact)
    ).

main(!IO) :-
    list.foldl((N::in, !.IO::di, !:IO::uo) :-
        factorial(N, FactN),
        io.format("Factorial of %d is: %s\n", [i(N), s(bigint.to_string(FactN))], !IO)
    , [5, 0, 20, 21], !IO),

    % Example error handling
    ( try [io(!IO)] (
        factorial(-3, _)
      )
      catch_any E ->
        io.write_string("Caught expected error: ", !IO),
        io.write(E, !IO),
        io.nl(!IO)
    ).
primeCheck:
:- module prime_check_demo.
:- interface.
:- import_module io.
:- pred main(io::di, io::uo) is det.

:- implementation.
:- import_module int, list, bool, math, string.

% Predicate to check if a number is prime. Succeeds if prime, fails otherwise.
% This uses semideterminism, common in Mercury for checks.
:- pred is_prime(int::in) is semidet.
is_prime(N) :-
    N > 1, % Rule 1: must be greater than 1
    ( if N =< 3 then % Rule 2: 2 and 3 are prime
        true
    else if N mod 2 = 0 then % Rule 3: exclude even numbers > 2
        fail
    else if N mod 3 = 0 then % Rule 4: exclude multiples of 3 > 3
        fail
    else
        % Rule 5: Check divisors from 5 up to sqrt(N), stepping by 6
        Limit = math.isqrt(N), % Integer square root
        is_prime_loop(5, Limit, N)
    ).

% Loop to check remaining potential divisors
:- pred is_prime_loop(int::in, int::in, int::in) is semidet.
is_prime_loop(I, Limit, N) :-
    ( if I > Limit then
        % No divisors found up to the limit, it's prime
        true
    else if N mod I = 0 then
        % Divisible by I, not prime
        fail
    else if N mod (I + 2) = 0 then
        % Divisible by I + 2, not prime
        fail
    else
        % Continue checking next pair of potential divisors
        is_prime_loop(I + 6, Limit, N)
    ).

main(!IO) :-
    NumbersToCheck = [2, 3, 4, 17, 25, 29, 97, 100, 1, -5, 1_000_000_007],

    io.write_string("--- Primality Test Results ---\n", !IO),
    list.foldl((N::in, !.IO::di, !:IO::uo) :-
        ( if is_prime(N) then
            io.format("%d is prime.\n", [i(N)], !IO)
        else
            io.format("%d is not prime.\n", [i(N)], !IO)
        )
    , NumbersToCheck, !IO).
readFile:
:- module read_file_demo.
:- interface.
:- import_module io.
:- pred main(io::di, io::uo) is det.

:- implementation.
:- import_module string, list, maybe, stream.file, stream, exception.

main(!IO) :-
    Filename = "mercury_read_test.txt",

    % --- Setup: Create dummy file ---
    io.open_output(Filename, MaybeOutStream, !IO),
    (
        MaybeOutStream = ok(OutStream),
        io.write_string("First line from Mercury.\n", OutStream, !IO),
        io.write_string("Second line with Unicode: äöüß\n", OutStream, !IO),
        io.close_output(OutStream, !IO),
        io.write_string("Created dummy file '" ++ Filename ++ "'\n", !IO)
    ;
        MaybeOutStream = error(Error),
        io.format("Error creating dummy file: %s\n", [s(io.error_message(Error))], !IO),
        exception.throw("Failed setup") % Abort if setup fails
    ),

    io.write_string("\n--- Reading file '" ++ Filename ++ "' ---\n", !IO),

    % Method 1: Read entire file as string
    io.read_file_as_string(Filename, MaybeFileContent, !IO),
    (
        MaybeFileContent = ok(Content),
        io.write_string("File content as single string:\n", !IO),
        io.write_string(Content, !IO), % Already includes newlines from file
        io.write_string("--- End of content ---\n", !IO)
    ;
        MaybeFileContent = error(Error),
        io.format("Error reading file '%s' as string: %s\n",
                  [s(Filename), s(io.error_message(Error))], !IO)
    ),

    % Method 2: Read file line by line
    io.write_string("\n--- Reading file line by line ---\n", !IO),
    io.open_input(Filename, MaybeInStream, !IO),
    (
        MaybeInStream = ok(InStream),
        read_lines_loop(InStream, 1, !IO), % Start reading lines
        io.close_input(InStream, !IO) % Close stream when done
    ;
        MaybeInStream = error(Error),
        io.format("Error opening file '%s' for line reading: %s\n",
                  [s(Filename), s(io.error_message(Error))], !IO)
    ),

    % --- Cleanup ---
    io.remove_file(Filename, MaybeRemoveRes, !IO),
    (
        MaybeRemoveRes = ok,
        io.write_string("\nCleaned up dummy file '" ++ Filename ++ "'.\n", !IO)
    ;
        MaybeRemoveRes = error(Error),
        io.format("\nError cleaning up dummy file: %s\n", [s(io.error_message(Error))], !IO)
    ).

% Helper to read lines recursively
:- pred read_lines_loop(io.text_input_stream::in, int::in, io::di, io::uo) is det.
read_lines_loop(Stream, LineNum, !IO) :-
    io.read_line_as_string(Stream, Result, !IO),
    (
        Result = ok(Line),
        io.format("Line %d: %s", [i(LineNum), s(Line)], !IO), % Line includes trailing newline
        read_lines_loop(Stream, LineNum + 1, !IO)
    ;
        Result = eof,
        io.write_string("--- End of file reached ---\n", !IO) % Done reading
    ;
        Result = error(Error),
        io.format("Error reading line %d: %s\n", [i(LineNum), s(io.error_message(Error))], !IO)
    ).
writeFile:
:- module write_file_demo.
:- interface.
:- import_module io.
:- pred main(io::di, io::uo) is det.

:- implementation.
:- import_module string, list, maybe, stream.file, stream, exception.

main(!IO) :-
    Filename = "output_mercury.txt",
    Line1 = "First line written using Mercury.",
    Line2 = "Second line with escaped chars: \t \\ \" ' $", % Escaped for Mercury string
    AppendLine = "This line was appended.",

    % --- Write using open_output (overwrite/create) ---
    io.write_string("--- Writing to '" ++ Filename ++ "' (overwrite) ---\n", !IO),
    io.open_output(Filename, MaybeOutStreamWrite, !IO),
    (
        MaybeOutStreamWrite = ok(OutStreamW),
        io.write_string(OutStreamW, Line1, !IO),
        io.nl(OutStreamW, !IO), % Write newline
        io.write_string(OutStreamW, Line2, !IO),
        io.nl(OutStreamW, !IO),
        io.format(OutStreamW, "Formatted line: %d, %s\n", [i(123), s("test")], !IO),
        io.close_output(OutStreamW, !IO), % Close the stream
        io.write_string("Successfully wrote initial content.\n", !IO)
    ;
        MaybeOutStreamWrite = error(Error),
        io.format("Error opening '%s' for writing: %s\n",
                  [s(Filename), s(io.error_message(Error))], !IO)
    ),

    % --- Append using open_append ---
    io.write_string("\n--- Appending to '" ++ Filename ++ "' ---\n", !IO),
    io.open_append(Filename, MaybeOutStreamAppend, !IO),
    (
        MaybeOutStreamAppend = ok(OutStreamA),
        io.write_string(OutStreamA, AppendLine, !IO),
        io.nl(OutStreamA, !IO),
        io.close_output(OutStreamA, !IO), % Close the stream
        io.write_string("Successfully appended content.\n", !IO)
    ;
        MaybeOutStreamAppend = error(Error),
        io.format("Error opening '%s' for appending: %s\n",
                  [s(Filename), s(io.error_message(Error))], !IO)
    ),

    % --- Display final content (by reading it back) ---
    io.write_string("\n--- Final content of '" ++ Filename ++ "': ---\n", !IO),
    io.read_file_as_string(Filename, MaybeContent, !IO),
    (
        MaybeContent = ok(Content),
        io.write_string(Content, !IO)
    ;
        MaybeContent = error(Error),
        io.format("Error reading back file: %s\n", [s(io.error_message(Error))], !IO)
    ).

    % Optional: Cleanup
    % io.remove_file(Filename, _, !IO).
fileExists:
:- module file_exists_demo.
:- interface.
:- import_module io.
:- pred main(io::di, io::uo) is det.

:- implementation.
:- import_module string, list, maybe, io.

main(!IO) :-
    FileToCheck = "mercury_exists_test.m", % Check for a file that likely exists if compiling
    DirToCheck = ".", % Current directory
    NonExistent = "no_such_mercury_thing.ext",

    % --- Setup: Maybe create a dummy file/dir if needed ---
    % (For simplicity, we check existing common paths)
    % io.open_output("dummy.tmp", Res, !IO), ( if Res = ok(S) then io.close_output(S, !IO) else true ),

    io.write_string("--- Checking Path Existence and Type ---\n", !IO),

    Paths = [FileToCheck, DirToCheck, NonExistent],

    list.foldl((Path::in, !.IO::di, !:IO::uo) :-
        io.write_string("Checking: '" ++ Path ++ "'\n", !IO),

        % io.check_file_accessibility(Path, [read], MaybeAccessRes, !IO),
        % ( MaybeAccessRes = ok -> io.write_string("  - Accessible (for read)\n", !IO)
        % ; MaybeAccessRes = error(E) -> io.format("  - Not accessible: %s\n", [s(io.error_message(E))], !IO)
        % ),

        io.file_type(Path, MaybeFileType, !IO),
        (
            MaybeFileType = ok(Type),
            ( Type = regular,      io.write_string("  - Type: Regular file\n", !IO)
            ; Type = directory,    io.write_string("  - Type: Directory\n", !IO)
            ; Type = symbolic_link,io.write_string("  - Type: Symbolic link\n", !IO)
            ; Type = named_pipe,   io.write_string("  - Type: Named pipe\n", !IO)
            ; Type = socket,       io.write_string("  - Type: Socket\n", !IO)
            ; Type = character_device, io.write_string("  - Type: Character device\n", !IO)
            ; Type = block_device, io.write_string("  - Type: Block device\n", !IO)
            ; Type = unknown,      io.write_string("  - Type: Unknown\n", !IO)
            )
        ;
            MaybeFileType = error(Error),
            ( if io.error_code(Error) = io.enoent then % Check if error is 'No such file or directory'
                io.write_string("  - Does not exist.\n", !IO)
            else
                io.format("  - Error checking type: %s\n", [s(io.error_message(Error))], !IO)
            )
        ),
        io.nl(!IO) % Add a blank line between checks
    , Paths, !IO).

    % Cleanup dummy if created
    % io.remove_file("dummy.tmp", _, !IO).
dateTime:
:- module date_time_demo.
:- interface.
:- import_module io.
:- pred main(io::di, io::uo) is det.

:- implementation.
:- import_module time, int, string.

main(!IO) :-
    io.write_string("--- Current Date and Time ---\n", !IO),

    % Get current time as seconds since epoch (UTC)
    time.time(TimeSinceEpoch, !IO),
    io.format("Seconds since epoch (UTC): %d\n", [i(TimeSinceEpoch)], !IO),

    % Convert epoch seconds to broken-down time structure (tm) for UTC
    time.gmtime(TimeSinceEpoch, TmUTC, !IO),
    io.write_string("Current Time (UTC):\n", !IO),
    print_tm(TmUTC, !IO),

    % Convert epoch seconds to broken-down time structure (tm) for Local Time
    time.localtime(TimeSinceEpoch, TmLocal, !IO),
    io.write_string("\nCurrent Time (Local):\n", !IO),
    print_tm(TmLocal, !IO),

    io.write_string("\n--- Formatting --- \n", !IO),
    % Format time using strftime (similar to C)
    % See Mercury docs or C strftime docs for format codes
    % Note: Needs double escaping for \ -> \\ for JS, then maybe more for C format? No, just JS.
    time.strftime(TmLocal, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", Formatted1, !IO),
    io.format("Formatted ('%%Y-%%m-%%d %%H:%%M:%%S'): %s\n", [s(Formatted1)], !IO),

    time.strftime(TmLocal, "%A, %B %d, %Y", Formatted2, !IO),
    io.format("Formatted ('%%A, %%B %%d, %%Y'): %s\n", [s(Formatted2)], !IO),

    time.strftime(TmLocal, "%c", FormattedLocale, !IO), % Locale's standard date and time
    io.format("Formatted (locale default '%%c'): %s\n", [s(FormattedLocale)], !IO),

    io.write_string("\n--- Parsing (strptime - Requires specific module/implementation) ---\n", !IO),
    % Mercury standard library doesn't have a direct strptime.
    % Parsing usually involves manual string processing or using libraries.
    io.write_string("Parsing from string not directly shown (requires more code).\n", !IO).


% Helper predicate to print the fields of a time.tm structure
:- pred print_tm(time.tm::in, io::di, io::uo) is det.
print_tm(Tm, !IO) :-
    io.format("  Year: %d (since 1900)\n", [i(Tm ^ tm_year)], !IO),
    io.format("  Month: %d (0-11)\n", [i(Tm ^ tm_mon)], !IO),
    io.format("  DayOfMonth: %d\n", [i(Tm ^ tm_mday)], !IO),
    io.format("  Hour: %d (0-23)\n", [i(Tm ^ tm_hour)], !IO),
    io.format("  Minute: %d (0-59)\n", [i(Tm ^ tm_min)], !IO),
    io.format("  Second: %d (0-60, incl leap)\n", [i(Tm ^ tm_sec)], !IO),
    io.format("  DayOfWeek: %d (0=Sun-6)\n", [i(Tm ^ tm_wday)], !IO),
    io.format("  DayOfYear: %d (0-365)\n", [i(Tm ^ tm_yday)], !IO),
    io.format("  IsDST: %d (-1=unknown, 0=no, >0=yes)\n", [i(Tm ^ tm_isdst)], !IO).
    % Note: tm_gmtoff and tm_zone are platform specific.
maxInArray:
:- module max_in_array_demo.
:- interface.
:- import_module io.
:- pred main(io::di, io::uo) is det.

:- implementation.
:- import_module list, int, float, string, maybe, require.

% Predicate to find the maximum element in a list.
% Returns 'maybe(T)' to handle the empty list case gracefully.
:- pred list_max(list(T)::in, maybe(T)::out) is det <= (order(T)).
list_max(List, MaybeMax) :-
    ( if List = [] then
        MaybeMax = no
    else if List = [Head | Tail] then
        list.foldl(max, Tail, Head, Max), % Find max starting with head
        MaybeMax = yes(Max)
    ).

% Function to find the maximum (unsafe, throws exception on empty list)
% :- func list_max_unsafe(list(T)) = T <= (order(T)).
% list_max_unsafe(List) = Max :-
%   require(unify(List, [_ | _]), "list_max_unsafe: empty list"),
%   List = [Head | Tail],
%   list.foldl(max, Tail, Head, Max).


main(!IO) :-
    Numbers = [10, 5, 42, 17, 8, 99, 32, -5, 0],
    io.format("Int List: %s\n", [s(string(Numbers))], !IO),
    list_max(Numbers, MaybeMaxInt),
    ( MaybeMaxInt = yes(MaxInt), io.format("Max Int: %d\n", [i(MaxInt)], !IO)
    ; MaybeMaxInt = no, io.write_string("List is empty.\n", !IO)
    ),

    EmptyList = list.empty : list(int),
    io.format("\nEmpty List: %s\n", [s(string(EmptyList))], !IO),
    list_max(EmptyList, MaybeMaxEmpty),
    ( MaybeMaxEmpty = yes(MaxEmpty), io.format("Max Empty: %d\n", [i(MaxEmpty)], !IO) % Should not happen
    ; MaybeMaxEmpty = no, io.write_string("List is empty (checked).\n", !IO)
    ),

    Floats = [1.5, -0.5, 3.14, 2.71, 3.14159, 3.14],
    io.format("\nFloat List: %s\n", [s(string(Floats))], !IO),
    list_max(Floats, MaybeMaxFloat),
    ( MaybeMaxFloat = yes(MaxFloat), io.format("Max Float: %f\n", [f(MaxFloat)], !IO)
    ; MaybeMaxFloat = no, io.write_string("List is empty.\n", !IO)
    ),

    Strings = ["apple", "banana", "Cherry", "Date", "Banana"],
    io.format("\nString List: %s\n", [s(string(Strings))], !IO),
    list_max(Strings, MaybeMaxString),
    ( MaybeMaxString = yes(MaxString), io.format("Max String (lex): %s\n", [s(MaxString)], !IO)
    ; MaybeMaxString = no, io.write_string("List is empty.\n", !IO)
    ).

    % Finding max based on a projection requires list.map + list_max or custom fold.
    % Example: Max by string length
    % StringLengths = list.map(string.length, Strings),
    % list_max(StringLengths, MaybeMaxLength), ... find corresponding string if needed.
